name: Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular trail running from Jaraba Sanctuary
name_original: Pinturas rupestres de la Roca Benedí y Cascada de Calmarza — trail running circular desde el Santuario de Jaraba
region_intro: Jogging routes around Jaraba traverse a landscape shaped by the dramatic Mesa River canyon, featuring impressive limestone walls and narrow gorges. The region is characterized by diverse natural environments, including gallery forests along riverbanks and scrubland with junipers and rosemary in higher areas. Pine forests also contribute to the varied scenery, offering a range of terrains for running. This area is recognized within the Natura 2000 network for its ecological significance. Best jogging routes around Jaraba The most popular jogging route is Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular trail running from Jaraba Sanctuary, a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features rock paintings and a waterfall. Another top favourite among local runners is Cerro de Somé and La Tranquera Reservoir Loop, a difficult 8.1 miles (13.1 km) path. This route offers views of La Tranquera Reservoir and includes varied terrain. Local runners also love the View of La Tranquera Reservoir loop from Nuévalos, a 4.1 miles (6.7 km) trail leading through the area surrounding La Tranquera Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour. Jogging around Jaraba is defined by the Mesa River canyon, gallery forests, and scrubland. The network of running routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Jaraba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Jaraba?
A: Komoot features 5 diverse running routes around Jaraba. These trails offer a range of experiences, from moderate jogs to more challenging runs, allowing you to explore the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Jaraba?
A: The jogging trails in Jaraba traverse a varied landscape. You'll encounter dramatic sections of the Mesa River canyon with impressive limestone walls, lush gallery forests along riverbanks, and scrubland with junipers and rosemary in higher areas. Pine forests also contribute to the diverse scenery, offering a mix of surfaces and elevations.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Jaraba?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, the region offers trails that can be adapted for families. For a less strenuous option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Tranquera Reservoir loop from Nuévalos&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.7 km trail. Always check the route details for elevation and distance to ensure it suits your family's fitness level.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in Jaraba?
A: Yes, Jaraba is known for its circular routes. The guide includes options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983229155/rock-paintings-of-roca-benedi-and-calmarza-waterfall-circular-trail-running-from-jaraba-sanctuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular trail running from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 16 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983232628/cerro-de-some-and-la-tranquera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Somé and La Tranquera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 13.1 km difficult route offering scenic views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Jaraba?
A: Jaraba's running routes offer stunning natural attractions. You can explore the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;Mesa River canyon&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its impressive vertical limestone walls. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983229155/rock-paintings-of-roca-benedi-and-calmarza-waterfall-circular-trail-running-from-jaraba-sanctuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; route, feature ancient rock art and waterfalls. Nearby, you can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba&lt;/a&gt;, and several waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caprichosa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Piedra Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, several routes provide beautiful views of water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983232628/cerro-de-some-and-la-tranquera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Somé and La Tranquera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views of La Tranquera Reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Tranquera Reservoir loop from Nuévalos&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through the area surrounding the reservoir. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5119833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirror Lake (Lago del Espejo)&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity.

Q: What is the best season for jogging in Jaraba?
A: Jaraba's diverse landscapes make it suitable for jogging across multiple seasons. The gallery forests along riverbanks provide shade, which can be pleasant during warmer months. The varied terrain, including scrubland and pine forests, offers different experiences depending on the season. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, ideal for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Jaraba?
A: While many routes in the guide are moderate to difficult, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Alhama de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.7 km trail with minimal elevation gain, making it a good option for beginners or those looking for a lighter run. Always check the route's elevation profile to match your comfort level.

Q: Can I expect to see any wildlife while running in Jaraba?
A: Yes, the Jaraba region is part of the Natura 2000 network, designated as a Special Protection Area for birds (ZEPA). As you jog through the &lt;strong&gt;Hoces del río Mesa (Mesa River Gorges)&lt;/strong&gt;, you might spot raptors soaring overhead, adding an element of wildlife observation to your run.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: For experienced trail runners seeking a challenge, Jaraba offers several longer and more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983229155/rock-paintings-of-roca-benedi-and-calmarza-waterfall-circular-trail-running-from-jaraba-sanctuary" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí and Calmarza Waterfall - circular trail running from Jaraba Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 16 km trail with significant elevation. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983232628/cerro-de-some-and-la-tranquera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de Somé and La Tranquera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an 13.1 km route with varied terrain.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of running routes in Jaraba?
A: The running routes around Jaraba cater to various fitness levels. The guide includes options ranging from moderate to difficult. You'll find 3 moderate routes and 2 difficult routes, ensuring there's a suitable challenge for most runners.

Q: Are there any running routes that start from nearby towns?
A: Yes, you can find running routes that originate from towns close to Jaraba. For example, there's a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cetina&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.6 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455195" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Alhama de Aragón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.7 km route.
tips:
The Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba is a place of great historical and spiritual interest, located in the stunning setting of the Barranco de la Hoz Seca. Built in the 18th century, the sanctuary is known for its architecture and natural setting.
It is said that the Virgin of Jaraba appeared on a rock oozing oil, giving it a miraculous quality.

The Roca Benedí cave paintings are one of the westernmost examples of Levantine rock art on the Iberian Peninsula. They are located within the Barranco de la Hoz Seca, near Jaraba, in the province of Zaragoza.
The paintings depict a hunting scene with two human figures (a man and a woman) and two animals, painted in black.
This is an essential corner for nature lovers. The "Pozo Redondo" waterfall in Calmarza is a charming place that is hidden in the lower part of the town where the Mesa River passes.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba
